title: Kalshi R Package
text:
R client for the Kalshi prediction market API.


Version: 0.1.0 Status: Functional, ready for use



R/
├── auth.R       # Authentication (RSA-PSS signing via system openssl)
├── utils.R      # Internal helpers (request building, pagination, date parsing)
├── markets.R    # Market data endpoints
├── events.R     # Events and series endpoints
├── portfolio.R  # Portfolio endpoints (balance, positions, orders, fills)
├── orders.R     # Trading endpoints (create, cancel, amend orders)
├── history.R    # Historical data analysis functions






Uses system openssl binary for RSA-PSS signing (R’s openssl package doesn’t expose PSS padding)
Private key path stored in internal environment, never read into R memory
API Key ID stored in .Renviron as KALSHI_API





Production: https://api.elections.kalshi.com
Demo: https://demo-api.elections.kalshi.com





All list endpoints return tibbles
Pagination handled automatically via kalshi_paginate()
Dates can be passed as strings (“2026-02-07”), Date objects, or Unix timestamps





httr2 - HTTP requests
cli - User-facing messages
purrr - Functional programming
tibble - Data frames
openssl - Key generation and base64 encoding
usethis - Environment file editing








kalshi_auth_setup(path) - Set private key path
generate_kalshi_keypair(path) - Generate RSA key pair
get_kalshi_access_token(env_var) - Get API key from environment
check_openssl_installed() - Verify system openssl
req_kalshi_auth(req) - Add auth headers to request





get_exchange_status() - Exchange operational status





get_markets(status, series_ticker, ...) - List markets with filters
get_market(ticker) - Single market details
get_orderbook(ticker, depth) - Order book
get_trades(ticker, limit, ...) - Public trades
get_candlesticks(ticker, series_ticker, start, end) - OHLC price data





get_series_list(category) - All series
get_series(series_ticker) - Single series details





get_events(status, series_ticker, ...) - List events
get_event(event_ticker) - Single event details
get_event_markets(event_ticker) - Markets for an event





get_balance() - Account balance
get_positions(ticker, ...) - Current positions
get_orders(status, ...) - Order history
get_fills(ticker, ...) - Trade fills
get_settlements(ticker, ...) - Settlement history





create_order(ticker, side, action, count, ...) - Place order
cancel_order(order_id) - Cancel order
amend_order(order_id, count, price) - Modify order
decrease_order(order_id, reduce_by) - Reduce order size
get_order(order_id) - Single order details
batch_create_orders(orders) - Create up to 20 orders
batch_cancel_orders(order_ids) - Cancel up to 20 orders
cancel_all_orders(ticker) - Cancel all resting orders





get_settled_markets(series_ticker, from, to) - Settled markets with date range
get_settled_events(series_ticker) - Settled events
get_series_history(series_ticker) - Full history for a series
get_event_results(series_ticker, date) - Event outcomes (simplified date input)
get_event_candlesticks(series_ticker, date) - Event OHLC data
get_market_history(ticker, series_ticker) - Market candles + trades
get_trade_history(ticker, ...) - Historical trades







library(kalshi)

# First time: generate key pair
generate_kalshi_keypair("kalshi-key.key")
# Upload public key to Kalshi dashboard, get Key ID

# Add to .Renviron: KALSHI_API=your-key-id
usethis::edit_r_environ()

# Each session: set key path
kalshi_auth_setup("kalshi-key.key")



# Get results for NYC temperature on a specific date
results <- get_event_results(series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY", date = "2026-02-07")

# See price evolution (hourly candles)
candles <- get_candlesticks(
  ticker = "KXHIGHNY-26FEB07-B26.5",
  series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY",
  start = "2026-02-06",
  end = "2026-02-08",
  period_interval = 60
)

# Get all historical results for a series
history <- get_series_history("KXHIGHNY")



# Place a limit order
order <- create_order(
  ticker = "KXHIGHNY-26FEB08-B26.5",
  side = "yes",
  action = "buy",
  count = 10,
  yes_price = 45
)

# Cancel it
cancel_order(order$order_id)




Built with qrtdown. Site at docs/index.html.
qrtdown::build_site()
qrtdown::preview_site()



devtools::check(remote = TRUE, manual = TRUE)




*.key - Private keys (in .gitignore)
.Renviron - Contains API key ID (in .gitignore)





KXHIGHNY - NYC high temperature
KXHIGHLA - LA high temperature
KXHIGHMIA - Miami high temperature
KXHIGHCHI - Chicago high temperature

title: Kalshi R Package
text:
library(kalshi)

# First time: generate key pair
generate_kalshi_keypair("kalshi-key.key")
# Upload public key to Kalshi dashboard, get Key ID

# Add to .Renviron: KALSHI_API=your-key-id
usethis::edit_r_environ()

# Each session: set key path
kalshi_auth_setup("kalshi-key.key")



# Get results for NYC temperature on a specific date
results <- get_event_results(series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY", date = "2026-02-07")

# See price evolution (hourly candles)
candles <- get_candlesticks(
  ticker = "KXHIGHNY-26FEB07-B26.5",
  series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY",
  start = "2026-02-06",
  end = "2026-02-08",
  period_interval = 60
)

# Get all historical results for a series
history <- get_series_history("KXHIGHNY")



# Place a limit order
order <- create_order(
  ticker = "KXHIGHNY-26FEB08-B26.5",
  side = "yes",
  action = "buy",
  count = 10,
  yes_price = 45
)

# Cancel it
cancel_order(order$order_id)

title: Kalshi R Package
text:
KXHIGHNY - NYC high temperature
KXHIGHLA - LA high temperature
KXHIGHMIA - Miami high temperature
KXHIGHCHI - Chicago high temperature

title: Authenticating with the Kalshi API
text: The kalshi package uses a secure, “Pure R” approach to authentication. Instead of relying on external Python libraries, it interfaces directly with your system’s openssl tool to perform RSA-PSS signing.

title: Authenticating with the Kalshi API
text:
Prerequisite: OpenSSL
This package requires the openssl command-line tool. Most Linux and macOS systems have this pre-installed. You can verify it by running:

library(kalshi)
check_openssl_installed()

title: Authenticating with the Kalshi API
text:
First-Time Setup: Generating Keys
If you do not already have an RSA key pair, generate one:

generate_kalshi_keypair("kalshi-key.key")

This creates a private .key file and displays the Public Key in your console.

Registering with Kalshi

Copy the Public Key printed in your console.
Log in to the Kalshi Developer Dashboard.
Click “Create API Key” and paste your public key.
Save the Key ID (a UUID) that Kalshi provides.

title: Authenticating with the Kalshi API
text:
Step 1: Environment Variables
Add your Key ID to your .Renviron file:

usethis::edit_r_environ()

Add this line:
KALSHI_API=your-uuid-here
Save and restart R.

title: Authenticating with the Kalshi API
text:
Step 2: Session Setup
Before making API calls, point the package to your private RSA key:

kalshi_auth_setup("kalshi-key.key")

title: Authenticating with the Kalshi API
text:
Making API Calls
Once authenticated, you can call any function:

# Get your account balance
bal <- get_balance()
cat("Balance: $", bal$balance / 100, "\n")

# Get market data
markets <- get_markets(status = "open", limit = 10)

# Get series information
series <- get_series_list(max_pages = 1)

title: Authenticating with the Kalshi API
text:
How Authentication Works
When you call a function like get_markets(): 1. Validation: check_kalshi_auth() ensures you’ve run the setup. 2. Signing: A message (timestamp + method + path) is signed using RSA-PSS via the system openssl binary. 3. Request: httr2 attaches the signature and your Key ID to the headers.

title: Authenticating with the Kalshi API
text:
Security Considerations

Key Safety: Your private key is never read into R memory; it is only accessed by the system OpenSSL tool.
Git Safety: Ensure *.key and .Renviron are in your .gitignore.

title: Authenticating with the Kalshi API
text:
Troubleshooting
If you see OpenSSL system call failed, verify the binary is available:
which openssl
openssl version
On Ubuntu/Debian:
sudo apt update && sudo apt install openssl
On macOS:
brew install openssl

title: req_kalshi_auth
text: < Back to Reference

title: req_kalshi_auth
text:
Usage
req_kalshi_auth(req, kalshi_access_token = "KALSHI_API")

title: req_kalshi_auth
text:
Arguments







Argument
Description




req
An ‘httr2’ request object.


kalshi_access_token
Character. The name of the environment variable containing your Kalshi Key ID. Defaults to ‘“KALSHI_API”’.

title: req_kalshi_auth
text:
Value
A modified ‘httr2’ request object with authentication headers.

title: req_kalshi_auth
text:
Description
Implements the Kalshi V2 API authentication protocol. Extracts metadata from an ‘httr2’ request, generates a SHA-256 RSA-PSS signature, and attaches the required headers.

title: req_kalshi_auth
text:
Details
The function signs the base path without query parameters, as required by Kalshi’s authentication scheme.

title: kalshi_request
text: < Back to Reference

title: kalshi_request
text:
Usage
kalshi_request(
  path,
  method = "GET",
  query = NULL,
  body = NULL,
  kalshi_access_token = "KALSHI_API",
  demo = FALSE
)

title: kalshi_request
text:
Arguments







Argument
Description




path
Character. The API endpoint path (e.g., “/trade-api/v2/markets”).


method
Character. HTTP method. Defaults to “GET”.


query
Named list. Query parameters to append.


body
List. Request body for POST/PUT requests.


kalshi_access_token
Character. Environment variable name for API key.


demo
Logical. Use demo environment if TRUE.

title: kalshi_request
text:
Value
An httr2 request object ready to perform.

title: kalshi_request
text:
Description
Internal helper to construct an authenticated httr2 request.

title: kalshi_paginate
text: < Back to Reference

title: kalshi_paginate
text:
Usage
kalshi_paginate(
  path,
  result_key,
  query = NULL,
  kalshi_access_token = "KALSHI_API",
  demo = FALSE,
  max_pages = NULL
)

title: kalshi_paginate
text:
Arguments







Argument
Description




path
Character. The API endpoint path.


result_key
Character. The key in the response containing results.


query
Named list. Additional query parameters.


kalshi_access_token
Character. Environment variable name for API key.


demo
Logical. Use demo environment if TRUE.


max_pages
Integer. Maximum number of pages to fetch. NULL for all.

title: kalshi_paginate
text:
Value
A list of all results across pages.

title: kalshi_paginate
text:
Description
Internal helper to handle cursor-based pagination.

title: kalshi_auth_setup
text: < Back to Reference

title: kalshi_auth_setup
text:
Usage
kalshi_auth_setup(path)

title: kalshi_auth_setup
text:
Arguments







Argument
Description




path
Character. The absolute or relative path to your ‘.key’ or ‘.pem’ file.

title: kalshi_auth_setup
text:
Value
Invisibly returns the normalized path.

title: kalshi_auth_setup
text:
Description
Configures the session by pointing the package to your private RSA key. This path is stored in an internal environment and used automatically for all subsequent API requests.

title: kalshi_auth_setup
text:
Examples
## Not run: 
kalshi_auth_setup("path/to/your/kalshi-key.key")

## End(Not run)

title: get_trade_history
text: < Back to Reference

title: get_trade_history
text:
Usage
get_trade_history(
  ticker = NULL,
  min_ts = NULL,
  max_ts = NULL,
  limit = 1000,
  max_pages = 10,
  demo = FALSE
)

title: get_trade_history
text:
Arguments



Argument
Description




ticker
Character. Filter by market ticker. Optional.


min_ts
Integer. Minimum timestamp (Unix ms).


max_ts
Integer. Maximum timestamp (Unix ms).


limit
Integer. Results per page (1-1000). Defaults to 1000.


max_pages
Integer. Maximum pages to fetch. Defaults to 10.


demo
Logical. Use demo environment if TRUE.

title: get_trade_history
text:
Value
A tibble with trade information.

title: get_trade_history
text:
Description
Retrieves all historical trades for a market or across all markets.

title: get_trade_history
text:
Examples
## Not run: 
# Get all trades for a market
trades <- get_trade_history(ticker = "KXHIGHNY-25FEB08-B54")

# Get recent trades across all markets
trades <- get_trade_history(max_pages = 5)

## End(Not run)

text:

Other history: get_event_candlesticks(), get_event_results(), get_market_history(), get_series_history(), get_settled_events(), get_settled_markets()

title: get_settled_markets
text: < Back to Reference

title: get_settled_markets
text:
Usage
get_settled_markets(
  series_ticker = NULL,
  event_ticker = NULL,
  from = NULL,
  to = NULL,
  limit = 200,
  max_pages = NULL,
  kalshi_access_token = "KALSHI_API",
  demo = FALSE
)

title: get_settled_markets
text:
Arguments







Argument
Description




series_ticker
Character. Filter by series ticker.


event_ticker
Character. Filter by event ticker.


from
Date, character, or integer. Start of date range. Accepts “2026-01-01”, as.Date(), or Unix seconds.


to
Date, character, or integer. End of date range. Accepts “2026-02-07”, as.Date(), or Unix seconds.


limit
Integer. Results per page (1-1000). Defaults to 200.


max_pages
Integer. Maximum pages to fetch. NULL for all.


kalshi_access_token
Character. Environment variable name for API key.


demo
Logical. Use demo environment if TRUE.

title: get_settled_markets
text:
Value
A tibble with settled market information including results.

title: get_settled_markets
text:
Description
Retrieves settled markets with optional date filtering. Useful for analyzing historical outcomes.

title: get_settled_markets
text:
Examples
## Not run: 
# Get all settled markets for a series
results <- get_settled_markets(series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY")

# Get markets settled in a date range
results <- get_settled_markets(
  series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY",
  from = "2026-01-01",
  to = "2026-02-01"
)

# Get markets settled in the last 7 days
results <- get_settled_markets(from = Sys.Date() - 7)

## End(Not run)

Retrieves all available series templates. A series represents a recurring event format (e.g., “Daily High Temperature in NYC”).

title: get_market_history
text: < Back to Reference

title: get_market_history
text:
Usage
get_market_history(
  ticker,
  series_ticker = NULL,
  include_candles = TRUE,
  include_trades = TRUE,
  period_interval = 60,
  kalshi_access_token = "KALSHI_API",
  demo = FALSE
)

title: get_market_history
text:
Arguments







Argument
Description




ticker
Character. The market ticker.


series_ticker
Character. The series ticker (required for candlesticks).


include_candles
Logical. Include candlestick data. Defaults to TRUE.


include_trades
Logical. Include trade data. Defaults to TRUE.


period_interval
Integer. Candle period in minutes: 1, 60, or 1440.


kalshi_access_token
Character. Environment variable name for API key.


demo
Logical. Use demo environment if TRUE.

title: get_market_history
text:
Value
A list with candles and trades tibbles.

title: get_market_history
text:
Description
Retrieves historical trade and price data for a specific market. Combines candlestick data with trade information.

title: get_market_history
text:
Examples
## Not run: 
history <- get_market_history(
  ticker = "KXHIGHNY-25FEB08-B54",
  series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY"
)
history$candles
history$trades

## End(Not run)

text:
See also

title: get_kalshi_access_token
text: < Back to Reference

title: get_kalshi_access_token
text:
Usage
get_kalshi_access_token(kalshi_access_token = "KALSHI_API")

title: get_kalshi_access_token
text:
Arguments







Argument
Description




kalshi_access_token
Character. The name of the environment variable where your Kalshi API Key ID is stored. Defaults to ‘“KALSHI_API”’.

title: get_kalshi_access_token
text:
Value
Returns the API Key ID string.

title: get_kalshi_access_token
text:
Description
Retrieves a stored Kalshi API Key ID from the system environment. If not found, prompts the user to edit their ‘.Renviron’ file.

title: get_kalshi_access_token
text:
Details
Storing API keys in your ‘.Renviron’ file is a best practice to avoid hard-coding secrets into your scripts.
To set your key: 1. Run ‘usethis::edit_r_environ()’ 2. Add a line: ‘KALSHI_API=your_key_id_here’ 3. Save and restart R.

title: get_kalshi_access_token
text:
Examples
## Not run: 
key_id <- get_kalshi_access_token()
key_id <- get_kalshi_access_token("MY_PROD_KEY")

## End(Not run)

title: get_kalshi_access_token
text:
See also

Argument

Value
A list with exchange status information:

exchange_active: Whether the exchange is operational
trading_active: Whether trading is currently enabled

title: get_exchange_status

title: get_event_results
text: < Back to Reference

title: get_event_results
text:
Usage
get_event_results(
  event_ticker = NULL,
  series_ticker = NULL,
  date = NULL,
  kalshi_access_token = "KALSHI_API",
  demo = FALSE
)

title: get_event_results
text:
Arguments







Argument
Description




event_ticker
Character. The event ticker (e.g., “KXHIGHNY-26FEB07”). If NULL, series_ticker and date must be provided.


series_ticker
Character. The series ticker (e.g., “KXHIGHNY”). Used with date parameter.


date
Date or character. The event date (e.g., “2026-02-07” or as.Date()). Used with series_ticker parameter.


kalshi_access_token
Character. Environment variable name for API key.


demo
Logical. Use demo environment if TRUE.

title: get_event_results
text:
Value
A tibble with market results for the event.

title: get_event_results
text:
Description
Retrieves all markets and their outcomes for a specific event. Accepts either a full event ticker or a series ticker with a date.

title: get_event_results
text:
Examples
## Not run: 
# Using full event ticker
results <- get_event_results("KXHIGHNY-26FEB07")

# Using series + date (simpler)
results <- get_event_results(series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY", date = "2026-02-07")

## End(Not run)

title: get_event_results
text:
See also
Other history: get_event_candlesticks(), get_market_history(), get_series_history(), get_settled_events(), get_settled_markets(), get_trade_history()

title: get_event_candlesticks
text:
Usage
get_event_candlesticks(
  event_ticker = NULL,
  series_ticker,
  date = NULL,
  period_interval = 60,
  start = NULL,
  end = NULL,
  kalshi_access_token = "KALSHI_API",
  demo = FALSE
)

title: get_event_candlesticks
text:
Arguments







Argument
Description




event_ticker
Character. The event ticker. If NULL, use series_ticker + date.


series_ticker
Character. The series ticker.


date
Date or character. The event date (used if event_ticker is NULL).


period_interval
Integer. Candle period in minutes: 1, 60, or 1440.


start
Date, character, or integer. Start date/timestamp.


end
Date, character, or integer. End date/timestamp.


kalshi_access_token
Character. Environment variable name for API key.


demo
Logical. Use demo environment if TRUE.

title: get_event_candlesticks
text:
Value
A tibble with OHLC data.

title: get_event_candlesticks
text:
Description
Retrieves aggregated historical OHLC data across all markets in an event.

title: get_event_candlesticks
text:
Examples
## Not run: 
# Using series + date
candles <- get_event_candlesticks(
  series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY",
  date = "2026-02-07",
  period_interval = 60
)

# Using full event ticker
candles <- get_event_candlesticks(
  event_ticker = "KXHIGHNY-26FEB07",
  series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY"
)

## End(Not run)

title: get_candlesticks
text: < Back to Reference

title: get_candlesticks
text:
Usage
get_candlesticks(
  ticker,
  series_ticker,
  period_interval = 60,
  start = NULL,
  end = NULL,
  demo = FALSE
)

title: get_candlesticks
text:
Arguments







Argument
Description




ticker
Character. The market ticker.


series_ticker
Character. The series ticker for this market.


period_interval
Integer. Candle period in minutes: 1, 60, or 1440.


start
Date, character, or integer. Start date/timestamp. Accepts “2026-02-01”, as.Date(), or Unix seconds. Defaults to 30 days ago.


end
Date, character, or integer. End date/timestamp. Accepts “2026-02-07”, as.Date(), or Unix seconds. Defaults to now.


demo
Logical. Use demo environment if TRUE.

title: get_candlesticks
text:
Value
A tibble with OHLC data: time, open, high, low, close, volume.

title: get_candlesticks
text:
Description
Retrieves historical OHLC price data for a specific market.

title: get_candlesticks
text:
Examples
## Not run: 
# Using dates (simple)
candles <- get_candlesticks(
  ticker = "KXHIGHNY-26FEB07-B26.5",
  series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY",
  start = "2026-02-06",
  end = "2026-02-08"
)

# Using period interval
candles <- get_candlesticks(
  ticker = "KXHIGHNY-26FEB07-B26.5",
  series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY",
  period_interval = 1  # 1-minute candles
)

## End(Not run)

title: get_candlesticks
text:
See also
Other markets: get_market(), get_markets(), get_orderbook(), get_trades()

title: generate_kalshi_keypair
text: < Back to Reference

title: generate_kalshi_keypair
text:
Usage
generate_kalshi_keypair(private_key_path = "kalshi-key.key")

title: generate_kalshi_keypair
text:
Arguments







Argument
Description




private_key_path
Character. The file path where the private key should be saved. Defaults to ‘“kalshi-key.key”’.

title: generate_kalshi_keypair
text:
Value
Returns the public key PEM string invisibly.

title: generate_kalshi_keypair
text:
Description
Generates a 2048-bit RSA key pair required for Kalshi’s V2 API authentication. Saves the private key to a local file and prints the public key to the console for uploading to the Kalshi Developer Dashboard.

title: generate_kalshi_keypair
text:
Details
Kalshi uses RSA-PSS signing for API requests. To use the API, you must: 1. Generate a key pair using this function. 2. Keep the ‘.key’ file secure (add it to your ‘.gitignore’). 3. Copy the public key text printed in the console. 4. Log into the Kalshi Dashboard and upload the public key to receive your Key ID.

title: generate_kalshi_keypair
text:
Examples
## Not run: 
generate_kalshi_keypair("my_kalshi_key.key")

## End(Not run)

title: generate_kalshi_keypair
text:
See also
Other authentication: check_openssl_installed(), get_kalshi_access_token()

title: create_order
text: < Back to Reference

title: create_order
text:
Usage
create_order(
  ticker,
  side,
  action,
  count,
  type = "limit",
  yes_price = NULL,
  no_price = NULL,
  expiration_ts = NULL,
  sell_position_floor = NULL,
  buy_max_cost = NULL,
  kalshi_access_token = "KALSHI_API",
  demo = FALSE
)

title: create_order
text:
Arguments







Argument
Description




ticker
Character. The market ticker.


side
Character. Order side: “yes” or “no”.


action
Character. Order action: “buy” or “sell”.


count
Integer. Number of contracts.


type
Character. Order type: “limit” or “market”. Defaults to “limit”.


yes_price
Integer. Price in cents (1-99) for yes contracts. Required for limit orders.


no_price
Integer. Price in cents (1-99) for no contracts. Alternative to yes_price.


expiration_ts
Integer. Unix timestamp for order expiration. Optional.


sell_position_floor
Integer. Minimum position to maintain when selling. Optional.


buy_max_cost
Integer. Maximum cost in cents for buy orders. Optional.


kalshi_access_token
Character. Environment variable name for API key.


demo
Logical. Use demo environment if TRUE.

title: create_order
text:
Value
A list with order details including order_id.

title: create_order
text:
Description
Creates a new order in a market. Orders can be limit or market orders, for either yes or no contracts.

title: create_order
text:
Examples
## Not run: 
# Buy 10 yes contracts at 45 cents
order <- create_order(
  ticker = "KXHIGHNY-25FEB08-B54",
  side = "yes",
  action = "buy",
  count = 10,
  yes_price = 45
)

# Sell 5 no contracts at 60 cents
order <- create_order(
  ticker = "KXHIGHNY-25FEB08-B54",
  side = "no",
  action = "sell",
  count = 5,
  no_price = 60
)

## End(Not run)

title: create_order
text:
See also
Other trading: amend_order(), batch_cancel_orders(), batch_create_orders(), cancel_all_orders(), cancel_order(), decrease_order(), get_order()

title: batch_create_orders
text: < Back to Reference

title: batch_create_orders
text:
Usage
batch_create_orders(orders, kalshi_access_token = "KALSHI_API", demo = FALSE)

title: batch_create_orders
text:
Arguments







Argument
Description




orders
List of order specifications. Each order should be a list with: ticker, side, action, count, type, and optionally yes_price/no_price.


kalshi_access_token
Character. Environment variable name for API key.


demo
Logical. Use demo environment if TRUE.

title: batch_create_orders
text:
Value
A list with created orders and any errors.

title: batch_create_orders
text:
Description
Creates multiple orders in a single request. Limited to 20 orders per batch.

title: batch_create_orders
text:
Examples
## Not run: 
orders <- list(
  list(
    ticker = "MARKET1", side = "yes", action = "buy",
    count = 10, type = "limit", yes_price = 45
  ),
  list(
    ticker = "MARKET2", side = "no", action = "buy",
    count = 5, type = "limit", no_price = 30
  )
)
result <- batch_create_orders(orders)

## End(Not run)

title: batch_create_orders
text:
See also
Other trading: amend_order(), batch_cancel_orders(), cancel_all_orders(), cancel_order(), create_order(), decrease_order(), get_order()

amend_order(
  order_id,
  count = NULL,
  yes_price = NULL,

)

title: amend_order
text:
Arguments







Argument
Description




order_id
Character. The order ID to amend.


count
Integer. New total contract count. Optional.


yes_price
Integer. New yes price in cents. Optional.


no_price
Integer. New no price in cents. Optional.


kalshi_access_token
Character. Environment variable name for API key.


demo
Logical. Use demo environment if TRUE.

title: amend_order
text:
Value
A list with the amended order details.

title: amend_order
text:
Description
Amends an existing resting order’s price and/or quantity.

title: amend_order
text:
Examples
## Not run: 
# Change price to 50 cents
amend_order("order-uuid", yes_price = 50)

# Change quantity to 20 contracts
amend_order("order-uuid", count = 20)

## End(Not run)

title: amend_order
text:
See also
Other trading: batch_cancel_orders(), batch_create_orders(), cancel_all_orders(), cancel_order(), create_order(), decrease_order(), get_order()

title: kalshi
text:
MIT + file LICENSE



Citing this package




Alejandro Hagan (author, maintainer)
R client for the Kalshi prediction market API.

title: kalshi
text:
Installation
# From source
devtools::install()

title: kalshi
text:
Quick Start
library(kalshi)
# Setup authentication (see Authentication section)
kalshi_auth_setup("kalshi-key.key")

# Check balance
get_balance()

# Get open markets
get_markets(status = "open", limit = 10)

# Get weather event results
get_event_results(series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY", date = "2026-02-07")

# Track price evolution
get_candlesticks(
  ticker = "KXHIGHNY-26FEB07-B26.5",
  series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY",
  start = "2026-02-06",
  end = "2026-02-08"
)

title: kalshi
text:
Authentication

Generate key pair:
generate_kalshi_keypair("kalshi-key.key")
Upload public key to Kalshi API Keys
Add Key ID to .Renviron:
KALSHI_API=your-key-id-here
Each session:
kalshi_auth_setup("kalshi-key.key")

title: kalshi
text:
Features

Market Data: Browse markets, series, events, orderbooks
Historical Analysis: Price history, settled markets, candlesticks
Portfolio: Balance, positions, orders, fills, settlements
Trading: Create, cancel, amend orders (single and batch)
Simplified Dates: Use "2026-02-07" instead of Unix timestamps

title: kalshi
text:
Documentation
qrtdown::preview_site()
Or see the docs/ folder.

title: kalshi
text:
License
MIT

title: get_events
text:
Arguments







Argument
Description




status
Character. Filter by status: “open”, “closed”, “settled”.


series_ticker
Character. Filter by series ticker.


with_nested_markets
Logical. Include nested market data.


limit
Integer. Results per page (1-200). Defaults to 100.


max_pages
Integer. Maximum pages to fetch. NULL for all.

Character. Environment variable name for API key.


demo
Logical. Use demo environment if TRUE.

title: get_events
text:
Value
A tibble with event information.

title: get_events
text:
Description
Retrieves a list of events with optional filtering. An event is a specific occurrence within a series (e.g., “NYC High Temperature on Feb 8, 2025”).

title: get_events
text:
Examples
## Not run: 
# Get all open events
events <- get_events(status = "open")

# Get events for a specific series

title: get_markets

Arguments







Argument
Description




status
Character. Filter by status: “unopened”, “open”, “closed”, “settled”.


series_ticker
Character. Filter by series ticker.


event_ticker
Character. Filter by event ticker.


tickers
Character vector. Specific market tickers to retrieve.


limit
Integer. Results per page (1-1000). Defaults to 100.


max_pages
Integer. Maximum pages to fetch. NULL for all. Defaults to NULL.


kalshi_access_token
Character. Environment variable name for API key.


demo
Logical. Use demo environment if TRUE.

text:
Value
A tibble with market information including ticker, title, status, yes_price, no_price, volume, and other market details.

title: get_markets
text:
Description
Retrieves a list of markets with optional filtering. Handles pagination automatically to return all matching results.

text:

## Not run:

# Get markets for a specific series
markets <- get_markets(series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY")

## End(Not run)

title: sign_kalshi_request
text: < Back to Reference

title: sign_kalshi_request
text:
Usage
sign_kalshi_request(timestamp, method, path)

title: sign_kalshi_request
text:
Arguments







Argument
Description




timestamp
Character. A millisecond Unix timestamp string.


method
Character. The HTTP method in uppercase (e.g., “GET”, “POST”).


path
Character. The API endpoint path without query parameters.

title: sign_kalshi_request
text:
Value
A base64-encoded string containing the RSA-PSS signature.

title: sign_kalshi_request
text:
Description
Performs RSA-PSS signing using the system’s ‘openssl’ binary to generate the ‘KALSHI-ACCESS-SIGNATURE’ header.

title: sign_kalshi_request
text:
Details
The R ‘openssl’ package does not expose RSA-PSS padding, so this function interfaces directly with the system’s ‘openssl’ installation.
Signature is generated using: - Message: ‘timestamp + method + path’ - Algorithm: SHA-256 - Padding: RSA-PSS - Salt Length: Equal to digest length (32 bytes)

title: Getting Started with kalshi
text: The kalshi package provides a complete R interface to the Kalshi prediction market API. This guide covers installation, authentication, and common use cases.

title: Getting Started with kalshi
text:
Installation

# Install from local source
devtools::install()

# Or load for development
devtools::load_all()

title: Getting Started with kalshi
text:
Authentication Setup
Kalshi uses RSA key-based authentication. You need to:

Generate a key pair
Upload the public key to Kalshi
Store your API Key ID


Step 1: Generate Keys

library(kalshi)

# Generate RSA key pair
generate_kalshi_keypair("kalshi-key.key")

This creates kalshi-key.key (private, keep secret) and prints the public key.


Step 2: Upload to Kalshi

Copy the public key from the console
Go to Kalshi API Keys
Create a new API key and paste your public key
Save the Key ID (a UUID like 5a2e774e-7f95-4bee-8e98-f4b29f5d38aa)



Step 3: Store Key ID

usethis::edit_r_environ()

Add this line to your .Renviron:
KALSHI_API=your-key-id-here
Restart R for changes to take effect.


Step 4: Authenticate Each Session

library(kalshi)
kalshi_auth_setup("kalshi-key.key")

title: Getting Started with kalshi
text:
Basic Usage

Check Account Balance

bal <- get_balance()
cat("Balance: $", bal$balance / 100, "\n")
cat("Portfolio value: $", bal$portfolio_value / 100, "\n")



Browse Markets

# Get open markets
markets <- get_markets(status = "open", limit = 10)

# Filter by series
weather_markets <- get_markets(series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY", status = "open")



Get Series Information

# List all series
series <- get_series_list()

# Find weather series
weather <- series[grepl("HIGH", series$ticker), ]

# Get specific series details
nyc <- get_series("KXHIGHNY")

title: Getting Started with kalshi
text:
Weather Market Analysis

Get Event Results

# Using simplified date input
results <- get_event_results(
  series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY",
  date = "2026-02-07"
)

# See which bracket won
results[results$result == "yes", c("title", "volume")]



Track Price Evolution

# Get hourly price history
candles <- get_candlesticks(
 ticker = "KXHIGHNY-26FEB07-B26.5",
  series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY",
  start = "2026-02-06",
  end = "2026-02-08",
  period_interval = 60  # hourly
)

# See the price journey
candles[, c("time", "open", "high", "low", "close", "volume")]

# Summary
cat("Started at:", candles$open[1], "%\n")
cat("Ended at:", candles$close[nrow(candles)], "%\n")



Historical Analysis

# Get all settled markets for a series
history <- get_series_history("KXHIGHNY")

# Get settled markets in a date range
recent <- get_settled_markets(
  series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY",
  from = "2026-01-01",
  to = "2026-02-01"
)

# Get markets from the last week
last_week <- get_settled_markets(from = Sys.Date() - 7)

title: Getting Started with kalshi
text:
Trading

Place Orders

# Buy 10 yes contracts at 45 cents
order <- create_order(
  ticker = "KXHIGHNY-26FEB08-B26.5",
  side = "yes",
  action = "buy",
  count = 10,
  yes_price = 45
)

# Market order (no price needed)
order <- create_order(
  ticker = "KXHIGHNY-26FEB08-B26.5",
  side = "yes",
  action = "buy",
  count = 10,
  type = "market"
)



Manage Orders

# View resting orders
orders <- get_orders(status = "resting")

# Cancel an order
cancel_order(order$order_id)

# Amend price
amend_order(order$order_id, yes_price = 50)

# Cancel all orders
cancel_all_orders()



View Portfolio

# Current positions
positions <- get_positions()

# Trade history
fills <- get_fills()

# Settlement history
settlements <- get_settlements()

title: Getting Started with kalshi
text:
Date Input Formats
All date parameters accept multiple formats:

# String dates
get_event_results(series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY", date = "2026-02-07")

# R Date objects
get_settled_markets(from = as.Date("2026-01-01"))

# Relative dates
get_settled_markets(from = Sys.Date() - 7)  # Last week

# Date ranges
get_candlesticks(
  ticker = "...",
  series_ticker = "...",
  start = "2026-02-01",
  end = "2026-02-07"
)

title: Getting Started with kalshi
text:
Available Weather Series



Series
Description




KXHIGHNY
NYC high temperature


KXHIGHLA
LA high temperature


KXHIGHMIA
Miami high temperature


KXHIGHCHI
Chicago high temperature



Find all series:

series <- get_series_list()

title: Getting Started with kalshi
text:
Error Handling
The package uses cli for informative error messages:

# If not authenticated
get_balance()
#> Error: Kalshi authentication has not been initialized.
#> ℹ You must provide the path to your private RSA key.
#> ✖ Please run: `kalshi_auth_setup()`.

# If key file missing
kalshi_auth_setup("nonexistent.key")
#> Error: The private key file was not found at: nonexistent.key

title: Getting Started with kalshi
text:
Function Reference
See the Reference page for complete documentation of all 37 exported functions.
